name: OpenShell Architecture
description: OpenShell is NVIDIA's secure sandboxed runtime for AI agents. Built in Rust (12 crates). Uses Tokio, Axum, gRPC, k3s, OPA/Rego policies, SQLite/Postgres, Ratatui TUI. Provides multi-layer isolation (Landlock, seccomp, network namespaces), policy-driven access control, HTTP CONNECT proxy with OPA evaluation, inference routing, SSH tunneling, and GPU passthrough.

name: OpenShell CLI Commands
description: Key command groups: openshell sandbox (create/list/delete/exec/connect/upload/download), openshell gateway (start/stop/destroy/add/login/select), openshell policy (set/get/list/delete/prove), openshell provider (create/get/list/update/delete), openshell inference (set/update/get), openshell forward (start/stop/list), openshell logs, openshell term (TUI), openshell doctor (logs/exec/check).

content: OpenShell is the foundational platform. NemoClaw depends on it. Provider types supported: claude, opencode, codex, copilot, generic, openai, anthropic, nvidia, gitlab, github, outlook.

name: OpenShell Input Inventory
description: ~106 unique inputs across all CLI commands. Sandbox (25): create with --name/--from/--gpu/--remote/--policy/--provider/--editor/--forward, exec, connect, upload, download. Gateway (15): start with --name/--port/--remote/--gpu/--recreate/--plaintext. Policy (15): set/get/list/delete/prove with YAML paths and revision numbers. Provider (10): create/update with --type (11 types) and --credential key=value pairs. Inference (8): set/update with --provider/--model/--system/--timeout. Plus forwarding, logs, settings, rules, diagnostics.

OpenShell 0.0.39 (currently blueprint-pinned) cannot attribute the peer binary of a proxied connection: it returns -(0) and the OPA engine fail-closes on an unresolved binary, so NO network policy rule can ever match. Every non-inference egress is denied even with a correct rule (pypi.org included); only inference.local works because the router special-cases it and skips attribution. HTTPS dies at NET:OPEN with 'failed to resolve peer binary: No ESTABLISHED TCP connection found for port'; HTTP resolves binary to - and FORWARD-denies. This makes Diffract UI host-approvals non-functional. Proven NOT a rule-format bug (a perfect hand-written rule with the binary listed still denies) and NOT a missing CAP_SYS_PTRACE (the supervisor already holds it).
resolution: Upgrade OpenShell to v0.0.57: verified in an isolated stack that attribution works (logs show ALLOWED /usr/bin/curl(PID), not -(0)) and approved hosts become reachable over HTTP and HTTPS. The bug is in the OpenShell compiled runtime, not fixable from the Diffract policy layer.

description: In the NemoClaw docker-driver deployment, OpenShell 0.0.39 has NO network-layer egress enforcement: the sandbox container keeps a direct internet route (default via the docker bridge) and the L7 proxy is consulted only because the agent env sets http_proxy. Any raw socket or proxy-unaware client egresses directly, bypassing deny-by-default entirely. Reproduced: curl with no proxy to example.com returned 200; the container even holds NET_ADMIN but no egress lockdown is applied. The policy is effectively advisory, not enforced.
resolution: v0.0.57 runs the workload in an isolated network namespace whose only egress is the proxy (the L4/nftables enforcement from 0.0.44/0.0.50). Supervisor logs 'Creating network namespace' and fails hard if it cannot (proxy mode requires isolation). Verified the bypass is closed in v0.0.57: direct and raw-socket egress are blocked. Sandbox base image must ship iproute2.
